reforms.description: The Italian government introduced the 'Bersani decree' in July 2006, a comprehensive liberalisation package aimed at enhancing competition in various sectors, including retail trade, professional services, and retail banking, by allowing year-round sales promotions and removing existing rules on market shares to foster innovation and productivity through price competition.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The liberalisation is expected to enhance competition, leading to increased innovation and productivity in the retail sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been enacted and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in the retail sector, promoting competition and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The 3 July 2006 decree law covers extensive measures to liberalise retail trade, professional services, retail banking and promote a new legal framework to boost competition in the non tradable sectors.

reforms.description: The Industry Law Decree (Industry2015) includes mechanisms to promote research through enhanced cooperation between Ministries, supporting research and venture capitalism via a Competitiveness Fund and a Fund for corporate Finance, alongside introducing fiscal incentives such as tax credits on research and grants to researchers to boost private equity and venture capital investment in small innovative firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The measures are designed to enhance research funding and stimulate private investment in innovation, which is expected to drive economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform introduces significant changes to the funding landscape for research and innovation in Italy.
reforms.source_quote: Industry Law Decree (Industry2015) includes general mechanisms to promote research through more cooperation between Ministries.

reforms.description: The 2007-09 Health Pact with the regions reinstates regional tax rate autonomy while requiring deficit regions to increase their taxes to the maximum allowable rates, aiming to control health spending and improve efficiency in the health sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: While the reform aims to improve efficiency in health spending, it may also create challenges for regions with deficits.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been enacted and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant change in the governance of health spending at the regional level.
reforms.source_quote: The 2007-09 Health Pact with the regions covering the period 2007-09 was entirely incorporated into the 2007 Budget Law.
